$ZETA is an AI-powered marketing intelligence platform built for a world where AI makes basic marketing content easier to create.

As content gets commoditized then the bottleneck shifts to knowing who to target, what to say, when to say it and how to automate the full customer journey.

Zeta helping OpenAI run advertising matters because it shows Athena and SuperGraph can help an AI-native company acquire, target and monetize users at scale.

Jeff Bezos said space-based data centers are “very realistic” but 2–3 year timelines are probably too aggressive.

He said the key constraints are energy, chip costs & launch costs even as Blue Origin pursues Project Sunrise with plans for up to 51,600 data-center satellites.

$MU said its financial outlook has strengthened since the last earnings call and it is on track for another record free cash flow quarter in fiscal Q3.

Micron also expects tightness across HBM, DRAM and NAND to continue well beyond calendar 2026.

Jeff Bezos said the bottom half of Americans should pay zero federal income tax.

He cited a nurse in Queens making ~$75K and paying ~$12K in taxes saying “we shouldn’t be asking this nurse in Queens to send money to Washington.”

📺 $SPY TESTS THE 8-DAY + $TSLA GETS TRICKY + $NVDA EARNINGS THOUGHTS

The recent market pullback is being massively overdramatized relative to the size of the prior rally. 

After a roughly 20% move off the lows, a decline from $722 down to $695 in $SPY over several sessions is being framed by many traders as “crazy” or “out of control,” but this is simply a healthy corrective phase inside a still-constructive trend.

*

From a technical perspective, $SPY briefly traded near $731, dipping slightly below the 8-day while still holding above the 21-day. 

That distinction matters because it suggests the broader trend remains intact even if short-term momentum cooled.

The key near-term level I'm watching now is the $737 area. If $SPY rallies back into $737 and gets rejected, the market could enter a choppy consolidation or “back-and-forth” environment rather than immediately resuming trend highs.

But again, this pullback is “methodical” rather than chaotic.

*

$TSLA has been one of the major winners in my portfolio during the recent rally, though trading it has become more difficult and volatile over the past several days.

Initially, I was accumulating Tesla through $TSLL and managed those trades successfully through the uptrend. As #TSLA approached a resistance area, I reduced exposure and took profits.

Even after trimming, I maintained smaller $TSLL positions and later became more aggressive again after the stock stabilized. 

Yesterday, I removed hedges tied to $TSLL and added back $TSLA stock directly. Tesla may not be acting as strongly as $MU and $SNDK recently, but I'm comfortable trimming some profits and evaluating how much of the opening gap can hold throughout today.

*

We have $NVDA earnings after the close today. 

Nvidia had pulled back from $236 to around $217 ahead of earnings, potentially creating what some traders might consider a more attractive setup for upside call spreads. 

You can even use the $235/$255 call spread, and if you were to trade #NVDA after-hours, it would need to reclaim and hold above $235 for momentum continuation.

I personally will not hold Nvidia options into earnings this time. I have no fear of missing out on Nvidia and do not feel the need to risk large amounts of money before a binary event just to potentially make some. 

Instead, I would rather wait and trade the stock after earnings once the print is out and volatility settles.

*

So, avoid panicking during normal pullbacks. Respect technical levels like the 8-day and 21-day moving averages and use hedges strategically instead of emotionally. 

Trim and trail winners rather than overstaying extended moves. Stay selective instead of forcing trades and avoid giving back too much profit after strong runs.

Markets are supposed to pull back after extended upside runs, and a few red days after six to seven weeks of upside is normal market behavior — not a sign of collapse.

*

Going to fast until the reverse carry trade hits.

The longest I’ve ever gone without food is 9 days.

Fuck it.

I will not relent.

I am always humbled when people who look to the left of the equal sign - who have followed my journey and come along for the ride - send me unsolicited words of encouragement.

There are good people out there.

Thank you.
Forward P/E: Russell 2000 at 18x. S&P 500 at 26x. A 31% discount. Before the pandemic, small caps traded at a premium. The gap has never been this wide.

You call me a broken clock, you get blocked.

I’m not broken.

And it’s a dumb analogy - not every minute of the day has the same level of importance.

There are weeks that define years.

Minutes that define days.

And on that Monday, August 5, 2024, as I had over 15 million organic impressions on my X account because I was the guy who “called it” when everyone was panicking I said that was likely the low (my impressions were the contrarian signals).

I turned bullish.
Bro. I have been here for the fuckening.
